[Swiss-Lib] Online Lecture: "Beyond the 3D Model: When Heritage Becomes Data, Evidence, and Memory

Sibille Christiane christiane.sibille at library.ethz.ch
Mon Jun 29 07:01:28 UTC 2026

  • Next message (by thread): [Swiss-Lib] Anmeldung + Programm: Herbstfortbildung 2026 der AKMB in Wien
  • Messages sorted by: [ date ] [ thread ] [ subject ] [ author ] 
We are pleased to invite you to our 𝗼𝗻𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗹𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗼𝗻 𝟯𝗗 𝗱𝗶𝗴𝗶𝘁𝗶𝘀𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗵𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗲𝘀 – part of Explore 3D, a joint open research data project by  <https://www.linkedin.com/company/epfl/> EPFL, the ETH Library <https://library.ethz.ch/> ,  <https://www.linkedin.com/company/universite-de-geneve/> Université de Genève and  <https://www.linkedin.com/company/university-of-lausanne-switzerland-/> University of Lausanne, and  <https://www.linkedin.com/company/dasch-swiss/> DaSCH - Swiss National Data and Service Center for the Humanities, Switzerland, funded by  <https://www.linkedin.com/company/swissuniversities/> swissuniversities.
 
"Beyond the 3D Model: When Heritage Becomes Data, Evidence, and Memory"
Speaker:  <https://www.linkedin.com/in/margaritaskamantzari/> Margarita Skamantzari



Thursday, 9 July 2026, 16:00 CEST
Online lecture (30–40 min + Q&A)
Register here:  <https://www.linkedin.com/safety/go/?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FecgzPMc4&urlhash=z_Ry&mt=rUVMaA5xb9DbXhg7J-YDQRG8Tvw25r5p-6Vd4-Q7WRriEBf-0gczu7J8s3-y0ftDGDmy_UmqaUjo8ln6m15Se1LXib4Vjs_raDAxdYfUnaUdL7XQEJYokQ&isSdui=true> https://lnkd.in/ecgzPMc4

What happens when cultural heritage is transformed into digital data, 3D models, archives, and immersive experiences? The lecture explores how 3D documentation can transform cultural heritage into a reusable resource for 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗽𝗿𝗲𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗿𝗶𝘀𝗸 𝗮𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝗻𝗴-𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗺 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻.
 
Margarita discusses how 3D heritage data can serve as evidence and memory, while raising practical and ethical questions around documentation, access and reuse within  <https://www.linkedin.com/search/results/all/?keywords=%23fair&origin=HASH_TAG_FROM_FEED> #FAIR and  <https://www.linkedin.com/search/results/all/?keywords=%23openresearchdata&origin=HASH_TAG_FROM_FEED> #OpenResearchData practices.
 
Drawing on field-based examples from heritage documentation, archaeological contexts, conflict-affected sites, risk assessment, and digital learning environments, the presentation reflects on the practical and ethical dimensions of creating, documenting, and sharing 3D heritage data within FAIR and open research data practices.

About the speaker:
Margarita Skamantzari is a Senior Researcher and PhD Candidate at the  <https://www.linkedin.com/company/national-technical-university-of-athens/> National Technical University of Athens, specialising in 3D cultural heritage documentation, digital heritage, and the use of documentation data for conservation and risk assessment, and serves as CIPA Expert Member and CIPA Emerging Professionals Co-chair.

 

ETH Zürich, ETH-Bibliothek, Sektion Sammlungen und Archive, Dr. Christiane Sibille, Digital Scholarship Services, Rämistrasse 101, CH-8092 Zürich

 <mailto:christiane.sibille at library.ethz.ch> christiane.sibille at library.ethz.ch,   <http://www.library.ethz.ch/> www.library.ethz.ch
